questions.statement:
<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the given bold word and mark your response accordingly.</span></p>

<p style="">Lawyers <strong>decried</strong> the imprisonment of several journalists.</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.</strong></p>

<div>Pliable</div>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence.</strong></p>

<p>My uncle is a colonel who gets <u>extremely angry</u> over petty issues every now and then.</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Strenuous</p>

<p><strong><span style="font-weight: 700;">Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words.</span></strong></p>

<p>A person who composes the steps of a dance</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Deficient</p>

<p><strong>Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.&nbsp;</strong></p>

<p>Using high-sounding words but with little meaning</p>

<p><strong>Among the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given sentence</strong></p>

<p>List of the business or subjects to be considered at a meeting</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Direction :</span>&nbsp;Identify the meaning of underlined idiomatic expressions from the alternatives given below them.</p>

<p>She <u>took exception to</u> Gaurav&rsquo;s insensitive remarks.</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Directions: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.</span></p>

<p style="">A person sent&nbsp;on a mission (usally official)</p>
